root_text.an5.68:1.1: “Pubbevāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sambodhā anabhisambuddho bodhisattova samāno pañca dhamme bhāvesiṁ, pañca dhamme bahulīkāsiṁ.
root_text.an5.68:1.8: So kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ ussoḷhipañcamānaṁ dhammānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā yassa yassa abhiññāsacchikaraṇīyassa dhammassa cittaṁ abhininnāmesiṁ abhiññāsacchikiriyāya,
root_text.an5.68:1.9: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇiṁ sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an5.68:2.1: So sace ākaṅkhiṁ: ‘anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhaveyyaṁ …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vatteyyan’ti,
root_text.an5.68:2.2: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇiṁ sati sati āyatane.
root_text.an5.68:3.1: So sace ākaṅkhiṁ …pe… ‘āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti,
root_text.an5.68:3.2: tatra tatreva sakkhibhabbataṁ pāpuṇiṁ sati sati āyatane”ti.

translation_text.an5.68:1.1: “Mendicants, before my awakening—when I was still not awake but intent on awakening—I developed and cultivated five things.
translation_text.an5.68:1.3: The basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort …
translation_text.an5.68:1.4: the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy, and active effort …
translation_text.an5.68:1.5: the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to mental development, and active effort …
translation_text.an5.68:1.6: the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to inquiry, and active effort.
translation_text.an5.68:1.7: And the fifth is sheer vigor.
translation_text.an5.68:1.8: When I had developed and cultivated these five things, with vigor as fifth,
translation_text.an5.68:1.9: I extended the mind to realize by insight each and every thing that can be realized by insight; and I was capable of realizing those things, since each and every one was within range.
translation_text.an5.68:2.1: If I wished: ‘May I multiply myself and become one again … controlling the body as far as the realm of divinity.’
translation_text.an5.68:2.2: I was capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.
translation_text.an5.68:3.1: If I wished: … ‘May I realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with my own insight due to the ending of defilements.’
translation_text.an5.68:3.2: I was capable of realizing it, since each and every one is within range.”
